_notes: PJM capacity market + cost-shift receipts. Annual data — update after each Base Residual Auction. All figures sourced; keep sources current.

auctions.note: +833% — sharpest jump in PJM history; Market Monitor: data centers drove 63% of the increase ($9.3B)
cleared at FERC-approved cap; uncapped simulation ~$388 ($141,828/MW-yr)
cap again; 6,623 MW SHORT of reliability requirement — first time ever

mechanisms: Shared capacity market: Ohio zones (ATSI/AEP/DEOK) and NoVA's Dominion zone clear in the same PJM auctions. Dominion's 20+ GW of forecast DC growth tightens the whole market; every Ohio ratepayer pays the clearing price.
Socialized transmission: ~$4.4B of DC-related transmission approved in 2024 alone is recovered from ratepayers across PJM, not just where the DCs are (UCS/IEEFA).
Import doubling: a Virginia-commissioned study found meeting tech forecasts would require doubling VA's imports from the rest of PJM — new generation and wires elsewhere, for load there.
Reliability erosion: 2027/28 cleared 6,623 MW below the one-in-ten standard. Record prices for a thinner margin.
counterpoint: AEP Ohio's large-load tariff (25MW+ DCs pay >=85% of contracted capacity monthly) is the emerging cost-containment model; FE has 2.6GW contracted by 2029 under its own large-load terms. Whether tariffs actually insulate residential ratepayers is an open, testable question — PUCO rate cases are the venue.
sources: PJM BRA results releases (pjm.com, Jul 2025 & Dec 2025)
IEEFA: 'Projected data center growth spurs PJM capacity prices by factor of 10'
